codeReview.summary: The feature successfully applies the requested Uniswap sizing/color/typography updates across the targeted UI components, but the commit also includes an out-of-scope edit to a generated Next.js file that should not be manually changed.

issues.description: Out-of-scope change committed to generated file (`import "./.next/dev/types/routes.d.ts"`). This file explicitly states it should not be edited, and the feature scope required CSS/Tailwind-only changes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: frontend-worker procedure requires running both typecheck and lint, but transcript evidence shows only typecheck was run while handoff marked followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/frontend-worker/SKILL.md (Work Procedure step 6) vs transcript skeleton for session 2f88a2ba-b4b5-4c5c-8d62-67d316b2750c (only `pnpm --filter web typecheck` shown).

sharedStateObservations.observation: frontend-worker skill asks to use agent-browser for visual verification but does not document concrete CLI usage; worker initially used an invalid command and had to discover usage via `--help`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows failed `agent-browser ... launch` (`Unknown command: launch`) followed by a separate help call before proceeding.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance does not explicitly remind workers to avoid committing framework-generated files (e.g., next-env/import-path churn), which allowed a non-feature edit to slip into the commit.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit b2d3a88 includes `web/next-env.d.ts` modification despite file comment: `NOTE: This file should not be edited`.

summary: Reviewed handoff, diff, transcript skeleton, and skill guidance for feature pixel-perfect-sizing-and-colors. UI spec changes are largely complete, but the commit fails review due to an out-of-scope generated-file modification in web/next-env.d.ts.
